title: Fig. 1 A in The effects of clays on bacterial community composition during arthropod decay
author: Corthésy, Nora
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Fig. 1 A Relative bacterial abundances at the phylum level in the presence of different clays. Each clay barplot includes one control (Ctl) consisting of sediment without shrimp and two marine shrimps (Sh1 and Sh2) decaying for two months. The blank represents contaminations during DNA extraction and manipulation. B Relative bacterial abundances at the class level in the presence of different clays. C Species level raw alpha diversity and Shannon diversity index in the presence of different clays in addition to NMDS analyses showing an overlap between bentonite and montmorillonite with both plotting separately from kaolinite
